steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content:
Sentence 1 states the general principle: random variables cluster around an average over many trials. Sentence 2 presents a specific coin-flip scenario (450–550 heads in 1,000 flips) as concrete evidence of that principle. The relationship is exemplification: sentence 2 illustrates sentence 1.
  • Choice A — ‘In addition,’: signals a new, separate point being added; sentence 2 is not a new point but an illustration of sentence 1. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘However,’: signals contrast or contradiction; sentence 2 does not contradict sentence 1. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘For instance,’: signals a specific example of a general claim; sentence 2 is exactly that — a concrete instance of the clustering principle. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘In comparison,’: signals a comparison between two distinct things; no comparison is being drawn here. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: “For instance” is the only transition that correctly signals exemplification, matching the relationship between the general principle and the coin-flip example. That confirms C is correct.
